response.justification: Wei Chen is fundamentally a JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js engineer whose C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month stint writing components for NI LabWindows (2014–2015) and a university thesis project — C++ does not even appear in the skills section. The role demands 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development, deep expertise in STL, multi-threading, and message-broker architectures, plus knowledge of IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards and industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, MQTT). None of these are evidenced in the resume. Additionally, the position is a Senior Manager role requiring team mentorship and ownership, for which no management track record is demonstrated.
strengths: Genuine high-performance, low-latency distributed-systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic using async I/O, fibers, and caching demonstrates relevant engineering mindset
Strong agile/TDD discipline (CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing at RIDE Capital) aligns with the role's agile and quality requirements
Complementary advantageous-skill coverage in React and Node.js, and prior event-driven architecture work on GISCollective
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, 2014–2015) and absent from the skills section; the entire career trajectory is web/TypeScript/Node.js, not systems programming
Zero evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or any industrial cybersecurity practice — a hard requirement for this role
No management or mentorship track record appropriate for a Senior Manager title, and no exposure to industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850)
